title: The Global Supply Chain of Waste
subtitle: Understanding how plastic waste travels from wealthy nations to developing countries and the legal frameworks attempting to manage this flow.
byline: Adapted from reporting by international environmental agencies and trade watchdogs, 2022-2024

summary.en: This article explores the international trade of plastic waste and the economic reasons behind it. It discusses how developed countries send their trash to developing nations for processing. We also look at the legal rules, such as the Basel Convention, that try to control this global movement.
summary.zh: 本文探討國際塑膠廢棄物貿易及其背後的經濟動機。文章討論已開發國家如何將垃圾運往開發中國家進行處理。我們也檢視了如《巴塞爾公約》等試圖規範全球廢棄物流動的法律框架。

paragraphs.en: According to the Basel Convention Secretariat, the international movement of plastic waste is now governed by strict legal frameworks designed to increase transparency. For many years, wealthy nations relied on global trade networks to export their plastic waste to developing countries, where labor costs for sorting and recycling were significantly lower. However, a 2022 briefing from the Basel Convention Secretariat indicates that new amendments have been implemented to ensure that such shipments are documented and managed safely. This shift represents a major change in how the global community views the responsibility of waste management, moving away from simple disposal toward a more accountable system.
paragraphs.zh: 根據巴塞爾公約秘書處的說法，國際塑膠廢棄物的流動現在受到旨在提高透明度的嚴格法律框架所規範。多年來，富裕國家依賴全球貿易網絡將塑膠廢棄物出口到開發中國家，因為當地的分類與回收勞動力成本顯著較低。然而，巴塞爾公約秘書處在 2022 年的一份簡報指出，新的修正案已經實施，以確保此類運輸經過記錄並獲得安全管理。這一轉變代表了全球社會看待廢棄物管理責任的方式發生了重大改變，從單純的棄置轉向更具責任感的系統。

paragraphs.en: Reports from the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) suggest that the primary economic driver for this trade is the cost difference between domestic recycling and international shipping. In many developed nations, the cost of labor and environmental compliance makes it expensive to process low-quality plastics locally. Consequently, as noted by UNEP, private companies often find it more profitable to bundle this waste and ship it to jurisdictions with less stringent environmental regulations. This economic reality has created a complex supply chain where plastic waste is treated as a commodity rather than a pollutant, often leading to unintended environmental consequences in receiving nations.
paragraphs.zh: 聯合國環境規劃署（UNEP）的報告指出，這項貿易的主要經濟驅動力在於國內回收與國際運輸之間的成本差異。在許多已開發國家，勞動力與環境合規的成本使得在當地處理低品質塑膠變得昂貴。因此，正如 UNEP 所指出的，私人企業通常發現將這些廢棄物打包並運往環境法規較寬鬆的司法管轄區更具獲利性。這種經濟現實創造了一個複雜的供應鏈，塑膠廢棄物被視為商品而非污染物，這往往導致接收國出現意想不到的環境後果。

paragraphs.en: According to the World Bank, developing nations often struggle to manage imported waste because they lack the necessary industrial infrastructure for high-volume processing. When massive quantities of plastic arrive at ports, local systems are frequently overwhelmed, leading to illegal dumping or open-air burning. The World Bank reports that without significant investment in waste management technology and local policy enforcement, the importation of foreign plastic waste often exacerbates existing environmental crises in these regions. This highlights the gap between the economic benefit of accepting plastic waste and the long-term ecological cost to the local communities that host these processing facilities.
paragraphs.zh: 根據世界銀行的說法，開發中國家往往難以管理進口的廢棄物，因為它們缺乏進行大量處理所需的工業基礎設施。當大量塑膠抵達港口時，當地系統經常不堪負荷，導致非法傾倒或露天焚燒。世界銀行報告指出，若沒有對廢棄物管理技術和當地政策執行進行重大投資，進口外國塑膠廢棄物往往會加劇這些地區現有的環境危機。這凸顯了接受塑膠廢棄物的經濟利益與這些處理設施所在地的當地社區所面臨的長期生態成本之間的差距。

paragraphs.en: The Basel Convention Secretariat explains that the legal framework for waste trade was significantly strengthened by the 'Plastic Waste Amendments' adopted in recent years. These rules mandate that countries must obtain 'prior informed consent' before shipping hazardous or difficult-to-recycle plastic waste to another nation. As the Basel Convention Secretariat notes, this requirement is intended to give importing countries the legal power to refuse shipments that they cannot manage safely. By requiring transparency at every stage of the transaction, international agreements aim to slow down the uncontrolled flow of plastic waste that has historically plagued many coastal and developing regions.
paragraphs.zh: 巴塞爾公約秘書處解釋說，近年來通過的「塑膠廢棄物修正案」顯著加強了廢棄物貿易的法律框架。這些規則規定，國家在將危險或難以回收的塑膠廢棄物運往另一個國家之前，必須獲得「事先知情同意」。正如巴塞爾公約秘書處所指出的，這項要求旨在賦予進口國拒絕其無法安全管理的貨運的法律權力。透過要求交易的每個階段保持透明，國際協議旨在減緩歷史上困擾許多沿海和開發中地區的塑膠廢棄物失控流動。

paragraphs.en: Research by environmental NGOs, as cited in recent trade studies, reveals that the definition of 'recyclable' is a major point of conflict in the global waste trade. Exporters often label mixed or contaminated plastic as 'recyclable' to bypass trade restrictions, even when the receiving country lacks the technology to process it. These studies suggest that this mislabeling is a common tactic used to move waste across borders under the guise of the circular economy. Consequently, international trade experts argue that stricter definitions and standardized testing are essential to stop the flow of non-recyclable materials into nations that are already struggling with their own waste production.
paragraphs.zh: 正如近期貿易研究中所引用的環境非政府組織研究顯示，「可回收」的定義是全球廢棄物貿易中的主要衝突點。出口商經常將混合或受污染的塑膠標記為「可回收」以規避貿易限制，即使接收國缺乏處理該塑膠的技術。這些研究認為，這種錯誤標記是為了在循環經濟的幌子下跨國轉移廢棄物的常見策略。因此，國際貿易專家認為，更嚴格的定義和標準化測試對於阻止不可回收材料流入那些已經在應對自身廢棄物生產問題的國家至關重要。

paragraphs.en: A 2023 report by the International Solid Waste Association (ISWA) emphasizes the role of the circular economy in reducing the need for international waste trade. By designing products that are easier to recycle and reducing the use of single-use plastics at the source, developed nations can decrease their reliance on exporting waste. The ISWA report suggests that a truly circular economy requires countries to take responsibility for their own waste, rather than outsourcing the environmental burden to other parts of the world. This approach shifts the focus from managing waste after it is created to preventing waste from being generated in the first place.
paragraphs.zh: 國際固體廢棄物協會（ISWA）2023 年的一份報告強調了循環經濟在減少國際廢棄物貿易需求方面的作用。透過設計更容易回收的產品並從源頭減少一次性塑膠的使用，已開發國家可以降低對出口廢棄物的依賴。ISWA 的報告建議，真正的循環經濟要求國家對自己的廢棄物負責，而不是將環境負擔外包給世界其他地區。這種方法將重點從廢棄物產生後的管理，轉向防止廢棄物在一開始就被製造出來。

paragraphs.en: According to the World Trade Organization (WTO), international cooperation is vital for balancing trade efficiency with environmental protection. As the WTO highlights in its policy discussions, the global supply chain of waste is deeply connected to broader issues of sustainability and economic development. Moving forward, experts say that nations must work together to create a system where plastic is kept within a closed loop, minimizing the need for international transit. By aligning national policies with global standards like the Basel Convention, the international community hopes to build a more sustainable future where waste is not a global commodity to be traded, but a resource to be managed locally.
paragraphs.zh: 根據世界貿易組織（WTO）的說法，國際合作對於平衡貿易效率與環境保護至關重要。正如 WTO 在其政策討論中所強調的，全球廢棄物供應鏈與更廣泛的永續發展與經濟發展議題密切相關。展望未來，專家表示各國必須共同努力建立一個將塑膠保持在封閉循環內的系統，以最大限度地減少國際運輸的需求。透過將國家政策與巴塞爾公約等全球標準掛鉤，國際社會希望建立一個更永續的未來，讓廢棄物不再是可供交易的全球商品，而是可在當地管理的資源。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The quality of being open and honest, making it easy to see how something is done.
keyTerms.noteZh: In this context, it refers to knowing exactly where waste is going and how it is being handled.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The official power to make legal decisions and judgments.
keyTerms.noteZh: Often used to refer to countries or regions with specific laws.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse.
keyTerms.noteZh: Often used with 'a crisis' or 'a problem'.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A formal procedure to ensure that a country is notified and agrees before receiving hazardous materials.
keyTerms.noteZh: A key legal term in the Basel Convention.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An economic system aimed at eliminating waste and the continual use of resources.

sources.name: Basel Convention Secretariat
sources.title: Basel Convention plastic waste amendments summary

quiz.question: What is the main requirement for countries shipping hazardous plastic waste under the updated Basel Convention?

options.A: They must pay a tax to the UN.
options.B: They must obtain 'prior informed consent' from the importing country.
options.C: They must recycle 100% of the plastic before shipping.
options.D: They must stop all plastic exports immediately.

quiz.explanationZh: 文章第四段提到，巴塞爾公約要求國家在運輸危險或難以回收的塑膠廢棄物前，必須獲得「事先知情同意」。

quiz.question: Why do some companies prefer to export waste to developing nations rather than recycling it domestically?

options.A: Developing nations have better recycling technology.
options.B: It is cheaper to ship waste than to pay for local labor and environmental compliance.
options.C: Developing nations have requested more plastic waste for their own industries.
options.D: International shipping is always free of charge.

quiz.explanationZh: 文章第二段指出，已開發國家的勞動力與環境合規成本高，因此將廢棄物運往法規較寬鬆的地區通常更具獲利性。

quiz.question: In the article, what does the word 'exacerbates' mean?

options.C: To make a bad situation worse.
options.D: To move something from one place to another.

quiz.explanationZh: Exacerbate 的意思是「惡化」或「加劇」，在文中指進口廢棄物讓當地的環境危機變得更嚴重。

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of the 'circular economy' as described in the article?

options.A: To increase the speed of global trade.
options.B: To make plastic waste cheaper to buy.
options.C: To reduce waste generation and keep resources in a closed loop.
options.D: To encourage countries to export their trash to more efficient nations.

quiz.explanationZh: 文章第六段解釋，循環經濟的目標是透過設計與源頭減量，減少廢棄物產生並讓資源保持在封閉循環中。

_seriesPartMeta.angle: The trade of plastic waste between developed and developing nations.
_seriesPartMeta.learningGoal: Identify the key stakeholders in the international waste trade and the economic drivers behind it.
keyQuestions: Where does plastic waste go after it is collected?
What role do international trade agreements play?
How do developing nations manage imported waste?

sourcePlan.searchHint: Basel Convention plastic waste amendments summary
sourcePlan.whyUseful: Explains the legal framework governing the movement of hazardous waste.
sourcePlan.whyUsefulZh: 解釋規範危險廢棄物跨境移動的法律框架。
sourcePlan.attributionExample: The Basel Convention Secretariat stated in a 2022 briefing that new amendments aim to improve transparency in waste trade.
